channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Platner Suspends Senate Bid in Maine After Rape Accusation
item.description: Mr. Platner’s withdrawal, which comes after a woman accused him of rape, creates deep uncertainty in a race that both parties see as crucial to their hopes of winning the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner, Lisa Lerer and Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times

item.title: ‘A Slow-Rolling Disaster’: Inside the Implosion of the Platner Campaign
item.description: Graham Platner’s bid for the Senate inspired progressive Democrats. But the campaign, which he suspended Wednesday, was messy, disorganized and ultimately doomed by a steady drip of scandal.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer, Katie Glueck and Michael Kruse
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times

item.title: Graham Platner Suspended His Senate Campaign. Here’s What’s Next for Democrats.
item.description: The deadline to pick a new nominee is July 27 and candidates are already lining up. State party leaders said they would hold some form of nominating convention.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Maine Democratic Party has less than three weeks to find a replacement to challenge Senator Susan Collins, the Republican incumbent.

item.title: Does a Sex Assault Claim Kill a Political Career? It Depends.
item.description: The swift exit of Graham Platner from the Maine Senate race followed Eric Swalwell’s similar exit from the California governor’s race, but Republicans have taken a different tack.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Democratic allies of Graham Platner called for him to end his campaign for Senate after a woman accused him of rape.

item.title: Tanker Attacks Risk Overplaying Iran’s Hand, Analysts Say
item.description: Iran’s history shows it is willing to dig in, even when it may suffer big losses.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Vessels on the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday. Traffic last weekend was about one-third of the prewar level of more than 100 ships daily, split evenly between the Iranian and Omani sides of the waterway.

item.title: Oil Prices Hold Gains After U.S. and Iran Trade Another Round of Strikes
item.description: Choppy trading in stocks and bonds also reflected anxiety among investors about the war’s effects on inflation and growth.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Issei Kato/Reuters

item.title: How U.S. Satellite Imagery Restrictions Are Changing How We Report on Iran
item.description: U.S. satellite image limitations have hampered journalists covering the Iran war. But other sources are offering workarounds, making it harder for militaries to conceal their actions.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Inspector of Buckling Building Cited for Missing Problems at Other Sites
item.description: A New York Times review of city records shows that the firm, Domani Inspection Services, was repeatedly accused of breaking New York City rules.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen, Dionne Searcey, Asmaa Elkeurti and Mihir Zaveri
item.category: Building and Construction Trade Council of Greater New York
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: An ambitious building project at 235 East 42nd Street in Manhattan was evacuated suddenly after workers noticed columns beginning to buckle.

item.title: Security Precaution Led Trump to Use Old Air Force One in Leaving Turkey
item.description: The Secret Service is said to have asked that the president not use the Qatari-donated jet when he left Ankara. The swap deepens questions about the retrofitting of the new plane.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Julian E. Barnes, Eric Schmitt and Eric Lipton
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: The older jet used as Air Force One as it was prepared for departure from Ankara, Turkey, on Wednesday.

item.title: After Insulting Allies at NATO Summit, Trump Says They Love Him
item.description: The meeting in Turkey featured President Trump at his most mercurial, veering from scorn to praise for European countries, including some whose patience with mollifying him has worn thin.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, on Wednesday.

item.title: License to Make Patriot Missiles Could Help Ukraine Defend Against Russia
item.description: With Patriot interceptors in short supply, President Trump’s statement that he would allow Ukraine to build them is a boon to Kyiv as it fights off Russian missile attacks. But it is just the start.
item.dc:creator: Constant Méheut, Nicholas Kulish and John Ismay
item.media:credit: Sergei Supinsky/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A Patriot air defense system in Ukraine, in 2024. President Volodymyr Zelensky has warned for weeks that his country was running out of interceptors for the system as Russia has stepped up attacks.

item.title: Which Country Has the Best-Dressed World Cup Fans?
item.description: At World Cup matches featuring teams from five continents, our photographer captured fans, flags, fringe, face paint and at least one horned helmet.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha
item.media:credit: Photographs by Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times

item.title: Judge Orders $5 Million Trump Judgment Be Released to E. Jean Carroll
item.description: Ms. Carroll had asked a federal judge to order the president to pay the judgment after a jury found he sexually abused and defamed her.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Benjamin Weiser
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Manhattan jury awarded the multimillion-dollar judgment to the writer E. Jean Carroll in May 2023.

item.title: 2026 Emmy Nominations: ‘The Pitt’ and ‘Hacks’ Lead the Pack
item.description: The HBO Max medical series earned 25 nominations in the drama category. And in comedy, the final season of “Hacks” set a record with 24 nominations.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin
item.category: Last Week Tonight With John Oliver (TV Program)

item.title: Emmys Snubs and Surprises 2026: Jon Hamm, Ariana Madix and More
item.description: Other unexpected results include nods for Chase Infiniti and the “Love Island USA” host Ariana Madix and a snub for “The Amazing Race.”
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale
item.media:credit: Jessica Kourkounis/Apple TV+, via Associated Press
item.media:description: “Your Friends & Neighbors,” the Apple TV suburban satire, was a surprise nominee for best drama, but the star, Jon Hamm, was snubbed.

item.title: Vance, in Wisconsin, Highlights Twin Fights, Against Fraud and Democrats
item.description: The vice president called Democrats “a party that is fighting for fraud,” even as he highlighted the case of a woman who was first indicted under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.media:credit: Mark Schiefelbein/Agence France-Presse, via Pool/Afp Via Getty Images
item.media:description: “This is not about better or worse policy,” Vice President JD Vance told a crowd in Milwaukee. “This is about who is supporting fraudsters and who is supporting you.”

item.title: As Progressives Rack Up Wins, Hakeem Jeffries Shrugs Off Possible Challenges
item.description: In a wide-ranging interview with The New York Times, Hakeem Jeffries, the House Democratic leader, said he didn’t consider the recent wave of anti-establishment victories “an overall critique of the party.”
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni and Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear Demands Health Update on Mitch McConnell
item.description: Senator Mitch McConnell has been hospitalized since June 14. Speculation about his health has spread in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky, middle, at a news conference in Lexington last month.

item.title: Family of Man Fatally Shot by ICE Agent Calls for Independent Inquiry
item.description: The man killed, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, was mourned by his sons as a father, husband and business owner. Details of the encounter remain unclear.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval
item.media:credit: Antranik Tavitian/Reuters
item.media:description: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo’s family members gathered for news conference in Houston after he was fatally shot by federal agents.

item.title: Trump Says He’ll Ask Supreme Court to Rehear Citizenship Case, an Unlikely Event
item.description: The last time the justices granted a rehearing request after a case decision was in 1965. The court has only once reversed itself after rehearing a case.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking at the White House on Monday.

item.title: As More Remains Are Found in Rubble From Venezuela Earthquakes, Horror Becomes Routine
item.description: Fearful that officials would tear down damaged buildings without accounting for any remaining bodies, Venezuelans are desperately digging for their relatives’ remains.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A priest and nuns blessing the rubble of a collapsed building in Catia La Mar on Tuesday.

item.title: Conor McGregor Is Said to Have Continued Use of Banned Drugs After Injury Healed
item.description: Conor McGregor, who will make his return to fighting this weekend, took human growth hormone and anabolic steroids after recovering from a broken leg, people with knowledge of the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: Conor McGregor in Washington last year. He is scheduled to return to fight on Saturday for the first time since breaking his leg in 2021.

item.title: How to Be a Good Guest at a Dinner Party
item.description: Tips for endearing yourself to your host and fellow attendees — and all but guaranteeing you’ll be asked back.
item.dc:creator: Rose Courteau

item.title: The Democrats Can’t Go On Like This
item.description: The trouble in Maine goes beyond a single candidate.
item.dc:creator: The Editorial Board
item.media:credit: Mark Peterson for The New York Times

item.title: The Zombification of America
item.description: Much of the world feared abandonment by the United States. It’s actually a new beginning.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Marche
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Late Night Joins the Hunt for Mitch McConnell
item.description: “Apparently, people don’t like a majority senator disappearing with no explanation for three weeks,” Ronny Chieng said on Wednesday’s “Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Second Person in a Week Killed by Federal Task Force in Memphis
item.description: Tennessee authorities are investigating both shootings involving agents working with the task force in Memphis.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Natalie Van Gundy/Daily Memphian, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A federal agent with the Memphis Safe Task Force shot and killed a person at a hotel in Memphis on Wednesday, according to the Tennessee Bureau of Investigation.
